what_it_does: This file is a sparse channel-attachment metadata record that declares an attachment id, its channel, an attachment_type of board_items, a target board:// URI, and an added timestamp.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no additional body or payload.
id follows the att-* naming convention.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item.
The file contains only linkage metadata and a provenance timestamp, with no attached content.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable id field.
References an external entity via a URI-style target field.
Records an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone in the added field.
concerns: The attachment has no body or payload, so the nature of what is attached is not described in this file.
The board:// target is opaque without additional context.
notes: A minimal channel-attachment stub that records a link from dragnet-development to board item item-ed0d24f1.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What is the semantic meaning of board://item-ed0d24f1?
Is the empty body intentional, or should there be an attached payload?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a board_items attachment; the actual content is only a metadata stub linking to a board item, with no attached item body.
intention_signals: It appears intended to record that a specific attachment exists in the dragnet-development channel and points to a particular board item, along with when it was added.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme as a target reference in an otherwise conventional attachment stub.
contribution: It contributes a persistent attachment-to-board-item reference and an audit timestamp that a fused system could use to reconstruct channel-board relationships.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata stub in dragnet-development of type board_items targeting board://item-ed0d24f1 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record the existence and provenance of a channel attachment that references a board item.
